file.content:
name: Node

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches-ignore: "dependabot/**"

permissions:
  contents: read # to fetch code (actions/checkout)

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.NPM_SCRIPT }} - ${{ matrix.NAME }} (${{ matrix.NODE_VERSION }})
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        NAME: ["Node"]
        NODE_VERSION: [20.x, 22.x, 24.x]
        NPM_SCRIPT: ["test:browserless"]
        include:
          - NAME: "Node"
            NODE_VERSION: "24.x"
            NPM_SCRIPT: "lint"
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.NODE_VERSION }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@6044e13b5dc448c55e2357c09f80417699197238 # v6.2.0
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: '**/package-lock.json'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build all for linting
        run: npm run build:all
        if: contains(matrix.NPM_SCRIPT, 'lint')

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm run ${{ matrix.NPM_SCRIPT }}

content.thinking:
The error is related to jsdom trying to use require() with an ES module. This is a Node.js version compatibility issue. Looking at the error, it seems the issue is that jsdom is trying to require parse5 which is now an ES module.

The issue is that the Node version I'm using (v22.11.0) doesn't match the requirements. Let me check the requirements again - they said:
```
"required: { node: '^20.19.0 || ^22.12.0 || >=24.0.0' }",
"current: { node: 'v22.11.0', npm: '10.9.0' }"
```

So Node.js 22.11.0 doesn't meet the requirement which is ^22.12.0 (22.12.0 or higher). I need to use a newer Node version. Let me update the Dockerfile to use Node.js 22.12.0 or higher.
